title: Identify Tipper Station Controls And Verify The Documented Tote Handling Flow
summary: Use the training material to identify the operator-facing controls at a tipper station and verify whether the observed station layout and tote handling flow match the documented clamp-tip-return sequence.
when_to_use: Use when an operator or reviewer needs to recognize the documented tipper station setup from the training source and compare the observed station arrangement or tote handling flow against the training description.
not_for: Detailed troubleshooting or fault recovery beyond identifying the documented HMI purpose and operator controls.
Inferring undocumented control meanings, corrective actions, or operating limits from this training segment alone.
Executing a production change, restart, or recovery procedure not explicitly described in this source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Visually identify the tipper station layout, operator-facing controls, and observed tote position or motion, then compare them to the documented training description and record mismatches.

safety_notes: The training source identifies an operator E-stop as part of the tipper station setup.
Do not infer undocumented control meanings or corrective actions from this segment alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the operator station
Training slide or source excerpt describing the tipper station
Visibility of the HMI, E-stop, ready-to-tip button, AGV tote position, and chute

access_required: Visual access to the operator station
Access to the referenced training slide or excerpt
system_preconditions: The tipper station and associated operator-facing controls are visible enough to compare against the training description.
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1, segment training_video_day1_segment_0008

visual_references.description: Training slide describing the tipper station layout, two tippers per station, tote clamp-tip-return flow, self-contained HMI, E-stop, and ready-to-tip button.

steps.title: Locate the operator station and identify the tipper setup
steps.purpose: Establish the documented station layout reference before checking controls or tote flow.
steps.instruction: Locate the operator station and identify the tipper setup. Confirm from the training source that each station is described as having two tippers.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is identified and the documented two-tipper station arrangement is recognized.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed station can be matched to the training description of an operator station with two tippers.
steps.failure_condition: The station layout cannot be matched to the documented two-tipper description or the setup is not visible enough to compare.

actions.label: Identify station layout
actions.exact_action: Locate the operator station and identify the tipper setup, noting from the source that each station has two tippers.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training slide statement that each operator station has two tippers and the overall tipper station depiction.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed station setup does not match the documented training description.
The station cannot be visually confirmed against the source material.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Each station has two tippers; tipper will clamp tote from AGV, actuates tipping arm to dump contents onto chute, and places tote back onto AGV.

steps.title: Identify the operator-facing controls
steps.purpose: Confirm which controls the training source explicitly associates with the tipper station.
steps.instruction: Identify the operator-facing controls called out in the source: the self-contained HMI, the E-stop, and the ready-to-tip button.
steps.expected_result: The documented HMI and operator controls are recognized at the station or in the source reference.
steps.healthy_condition: The self-contained HMI, E-stop, and ready-to-tip button are identifiable from the source and can be compared to what is present at the station.
steps.failure_condition: One or more documented controls cannot be identified or the observed controls do not match the training description.

actions.label: Identify HMI
actions.exact_action: Identify the self-contained HMI referenced in the source for operation and fault recovery.

actions.label: Identify safety and tip controls
actions.exact_action: Identify the E-stop and ready-to-tip button called out in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text describing the self-contained HMI for operation and fault recovery and the operator E-stop and ready-to-tip button.

stop_or_escalate_if: The station controls do not match the documented training description.
A control is present but its meaning would require inference beyond what the source states.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Self-contained HMI for operation and fault recovery.
Operators equipped with Estop and ready to tip buttons.

steps.title: Observe tote position for tipper handling
steps.purpose: Check whether a tote from the AGV is positioned in a way that aligns with the documented tipper handling context.
steps.instruction: Observe whether a tote from the AGV is positioned for tipper handling.
steps.expected_result: The tote position relative to the AGV and tipper can be observed and compared to the documented handling flow.
steps.healthy_condition: A tote from the AGV is visible or the station state can be reasonably compared to the documented AGV-to-tipper handling context.
steps.failure_condition: The tote position cannot be observed or the observed setup does not align with the documented AGV-to-tipper handling context.

actions.label: Observe tote position
actions.exact_action: Observe whether a tote from the AGV is positioned for tipper handling.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide description that the tipper clamps a tote from the AGV and returns the tote back onto the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote or AGV relationship at the station does not match the documented training description.
The tote position cannot be verified visually.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Each station has two tippers; tipper will clamp tote from AGV, actuates tipping arm to dump contents onto chute, and places tote back onto AGV.

source_evidence.summary: Tipper component reference: clamps tote from AGV, actuates tipping arm, dumps contents onto chute, and returns tote to AGV.

steps.title: Verify the documented clamp-tip-return sequence
steps.purpose: Compare the observed station behavior or arrangement to the training source's documented tote handling sequence.
steps.instruction: Verify the documented motion sequence against what is present at the station: the tipper clamps the tote from the AGV, the tipping arm dumps contents onto the chute, and the tote is placed back onto the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The observed station behavior or arrangement is confirmed as matching or not matching the documented clamp-tip-return flow.
steps.healthy_condition: The station matches the documented sequence of clamp from AGV, tip to chute, and return tote to AGV.
steps.failure_condition: Any part of the documented sequence is absent, contradicted, or appears inconsistent with the observed station setup or motion.

actions.label: Compare handling sequence
actions.exact_action: Verify the documented motion sequence against what is present at the station: the tipper clamps the tote from the AGV, the tipping arm dumps contents onto the chute, and the tote is placed back onto the AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text describing tote clamping from the AGV, tipping contents onto the chute, and placing the tote back onto the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The handling sequence does not match the documented training description.
Observed motion suggests a different sequence than clamp, tip to chute, and return to AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Each station has two tippers; tipper will clamp tote from AGV, actuates tipping arm to dump contents onto chute, and places tote back onto AGV.

source_evidence.summary: Each operator station has two tippers used to receive a tote from an AGV, clamp it, tip contents onto a chute, and return the tote.

steps.title: Record mismatches to the documented description
steps.purpose: Capture any discrepancy between the observed station and the training reference without extending beyond source-supported guidance.
steps.instruction: Record any mismatch between the observed station setup or motion and the documented training description, without inventing corrective actions.
steps.expected_result: Any mismatch is documented for follow-up or escalation.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed setup and motion either match the documented description or any mismatch is clearly recorded.
steps.failure_condition: A mismatch exists but is not recorded, or corrective actions are inferred without source support.

actions.label: Document mismatch
actions.exact_action: Record any mismatch between the observed station setup or motion and the documented training description, without inventing corrective actions.

stop_or_escalate_if: The station controls or handling sequence do not match the documented training description.
Additional interpretation or corrective action would require information not present in this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Training segment supports identifying controls and comparing observed flow to the documented sequence, but not detailed troubleshooting.

source_evidence.source_note: Escalate if the station controls or handling sequence do not match the documented training description.
Do not infer undocumented control meanings or corrective actions from this segment alone.

success_criteria: The operator-facing controls identified at the station match the documented source references for the self-contained HMI, E-stop, and ready-to-tip button.
The observed station layout matches the documented description that each operator station has two tippers.
The observed tote handling flow matches the documented sequence: clamp tote from AGV, tip contents onto chute, and place tote back onto AGV.
Any mismatch is recorded without adding unsupported corrective actions.
healthy_conditions: The station can be recognized as the documented tipper setup.
The self-contained HMI is identifiable as the source-referenced interface for operation and fault recovery.
The E-stop and ready-to-tip button are identifiable as documented operator controls.
The tote handling sequence aligns with the documented clamp-tip-return flow.
failure_conditions: The station layout does not match the documented two-tipper operator station description.
The documented controls cannot be identified or do not match what is present.
The observed tote handling sequence does not match the documented clamp-tip-return flow.
The reviewer would need to infer undocumented meanings or corrective actions to continue.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the station controls or handling sequence do not match the documented training description.
Escalate when the observed station cannot be confidently compared to the source using only the provided training evidence.
Do not propose corrective actions from this source alone; request additional procedure or SME guidance if needed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall tipper station reference slide showing two tippers per station, tote clamp-tip-return flow, HMI, E-stop, and ready-to-tip button.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Each station has two tippers; tipper will clamp tote from AGV, actuates tipping arm to dump contents onto chute, and places tote back onto AGV.
Self-contained HMI for operation and fault recovery.
Operators equipped with Estop and ready to tip buttons.

missing_details: The source is a training overview and does not provide a detailed operating sequence for pressing controls or initiating a tip.
The source does not provide timing expectations, acceptance tolerances, or corrective actions.
The source does not define escalation contacts or downstream documentation requirements for recorded mismatches.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required for any mismatch condition.
